# flashblade_tls_policy (Data Source)

Reads an existing FlashBlade TLS policy by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "flashblade_tls_policy" "example" {
  name = "strict-tls"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the TLS policy to look up.

### Read-Only

- `appliance_certificate` (String) The name of the certificate used by the appliance for TLS connections.
- `client_certificates_required` (Boolean) When true, clients must present a certificate for mTLS.
- `disabled_tls_ciphers` (List of String) List of TLS cipher suites that are disabled.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the TLS policy is enabled.
- `enabled_tls_ciphers` (List of String) List of explicitly enabled TLS cipher suites.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the TLS policy.
- `is_local` (Boolean) Whether this TLS policy is local to the array.
- `min_tls_version` (String) The minimum TLS version required (e.g. TLSv1.2, TLSv1.3).
- `policy_type` (String) The type of the TLS policy.
- `trusted_client_certificate_authority` (String) The name of the certificate authority used to verify client certificates for mTLS.
- `verify_client_certificate_trust` (Boolean) When true, client certificates are verified against the trusted CA.
